core_mechanism: modelling one trajectory as a rigid space-time translation of another — the follower signal equals the leader signal shifted by a constant time lag and a constant spatial offset — with the two shift parameters recovered by minimizing the variance of the residual gap under that translation, plus an endpoint/boundary correction for the finite-interval transitional regimes
brief_reason: The abstract's 'trajectory-translation principle' and 'shifting optimization that estimates the optimal parameters by minimizing spacing variance' describe recovering a constant lag-and-offset translation aligning one trajectory to another, with a boundary correction added for interaction endpoints.
